value.reason: The cited page explicitly documents Claude Code's OpenTelemetry monitoring feature, covering metrics, events/logs, and traces exported via OTLP to compatible backends (Jaeger, Datadog, Honeycomb are common OTLP backends). The page is clearly about audit logging/telemetry — recording agent actions (tool activity, usage, costs) for observability and compliance — distinct from the adjacent capabilities listed. The label "OpenTelemetry Monitoring" matches the page's feature name and heading. The description accurately reflects the page: OTLP export of metrics, events, and traces; configurable endpoint; captures agent actions for observability and compliance review. The mention of cost/usage tracking in the page is secondary context and does not make this a 'Cost & usage tracking' capability — the primary feature documented is the OTel telemetry pipeline for agent-action recording.
